message:
feat: add asupersync-runtime feature-gated mux subscription loop and simplify runtime compat calls

Refactor the mux client's pane output subscription to support the
asupersync async runtime alongside the default tokio runtime via
compile-time feature flags.

mux_client.rs:
- Add feature-gated pane_delta_recv_with_cx() for asupersync-runtime
  that passes the Cx context to receiver operations, alongside the
  existing pane_delta_recv() for the tokio path
- Import RuntimeHandle under asupersync-runtime feature flag
- Replace runtime-abstracted wrapper calls with direct runtime API
  calls: mpsc_recv_option → rx.recv() (tokio) / rx.recv(cx) (asupersync),
  watch_borrow_and_update_clone → borrow_and_clone / borrow_and_update,
  watch_changed → changed() / changed(cx)
- Add complete feature-gated run_subscription_loop() for
  asupersync-runtime that threads Cx through the entire poll loop
  including cancel_requested, pane_delta_recv, and
  wait_for_cancel_change operations
- Add feature-gated wait_for_cancel_change_with_cx() for
  asupersync-runtime cancel signal monitoring
- Separate cancel_requested() into feature-gated branches using
  borrow_and_clone (asupersync) vs borrow_and_update (tokio)
- Remove now-unused runtime_compat imports (mpsc_recv_option,
  watch_borrow_and_update_clone, watch_changed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
